decks.name: English Riddles — Débutant
decks.description: 15 énigmes : trouvez le mot anglais grâce à des indices simples, en anglais.

clues: I am an animal.
I live with people.
I love to play and run.
I wag my tail when I am happy.
I say "woof woof".
I am a small animal.
I sleep a lot during the day.
I like to catch mice.
I purr when you pet me.
I say "meow".
I am a fruit.
I grow on a tree.
I can be red, green or yellow.
Children sometimes give me to their teacher.
One of me a day keeps the doctor away.
You can see me in the sky.
I am very, very hot.
I give light to the Earth.
I rise in the morning and set in the evening.
I am the yellow star at the center of our solar system.
You drink me every day.
I have no color and no taste.
Fish live in me.
I fall from the sky as rain.
I fill the seas, lakes and rivers.
People live in me.
I can be big or small, made of wood or brick.
I have a roof, a door and windows.
You sleep, eat and cook inside me.
You open my front door when you come home.
You can hold me in your hands.
I am full of pages.
I tell stories or teach you things.
You can borrow me from a library.
You read me.
You can eat me.
I am made from flour.
The baker makes me every morning.
The French baguette is a famous kind of me.
You use two slices of me to make a sandwich.
I come from the clouds.
I make everything wet.
Plants love me.
You need an umbrella when I fall.
I am water falling from the sky.
You wear me every day.
I always come in pairs.
You put me on before going outside.
I protect your feet.
You tie my laces.
I am a drink.
I am white.
Babies love me.
I come from a cow.
You pour me on your breakfast cereal.
I am tall and I live a long time.
Birds make their nests in me.
I have leaves and branches.
I lose my leaves in autumn.
I am made of wood and I grow in the forest.
You see me at night.
I change shape during the month.
I go around the Earth.
Astronauts walked on me in 1969.
I shine in the dark sky, next to the stars.
I am oval.
I am white or brown outside, yellow inside.
The hen makes me.
You can boil me or fry me.
You eat me for breakfast, sometimes with bacon.
I am a piece of furniture.
I have four legs but I cannot walk.
You find me around a table.
I have a back but no head.
You sit on me.
